artifact_identity.series_role: canonical constraint packet for later repo-structure discovery, design, migration-planning, and decision prompts; downstream of stronger canon, planning law, audit evidence, and live implementation evidence
artifact_identity.replacement_target: later explicit repo-structure follow-up after new playable-baseline evidence or an approved redesign checkpoint

verified_now.statement: Post-Zeta doctrine and gating closure is complete, no further internal Zeta prompt is required, and the next broader mode is reconciliation and consolidation rather than more internal Zeta expansion.

The XStack/AIDE narrowing-and-contract phase is complete, no further XStack/AIDE prompt is required before the playable baseline exists, and the existing X-series artifacts act as a constraint packet rather than a competing implementation track.
CMakePresets.json still defines aligned verify configure, build, and test presets, and the audit freezes that lane as the canonical compiled baseline.
tool_run_validation.py and testx_all.py both expose FAST/STRICT/FULL profile surfaces, with FAST frozen as the minimum honest validation lane.
The Python/AppShell launcher and setup shells are real repo-rooted operator surfaces and remain the strongest repo-local command surfaces in the audit packet.

SessionX create/boot surfaces, the local singleplayer controller, deterministic process spawn helper, and loopback transport adapter together define a real local authority path, even though parts of that path remain fragile.
The authored MVP baseline asset trio exists and is live: profile.bundle.mvp_default, pack_lock.mvp_default, and session.mvp_default.

likely_but_not_fully_verified.statement: The current strongest runnable compiled path is still the out/build/vs2026/verify tree produced by the verify preset family.
The strongest repo-local product-shell path remains Python/AppShell-first rather than native-wrapper-first.
The compiled verify tree still carries broad CTest coverage, including playtest-adjacent suites, but that breadth was not fully re-executed in this prompt.
The shortest path to a playable baseline is still wrapper-hardening around existing session creation plus local loopback authority rather than greenfield runtime or platform work.

blocked_or_fragile.statement: server/server_main.py remains an unsafe public Python server startup path because its REPO_ROOT_HINT still resolves outside the repo root.
session_boot remains coupled to sessionx.runner, and runner.py still resolves boot artifacts back through repo_root/saves/<save_id> instead of honoring alternate save roots end to end.
tools/mvp/runtime_entry.py --local-singleplayer remains transitional and does not yet qualify as the fully hardened public playtest command.
Launcher-supervised attach and restart behavior remains fragile enough that the audit treats supervision stability as a real blocker for repeatable internal sessions.
External multiplayer transport remains blocked because the non-loopback transport surfaces are still explicit stubs.
Native launcher and setup shells remain wrapper-thin and stub-heavy relative to the Python/AppShell shells.

doctrinally_closed_but_realization_incom.statement: Post-Zeta frontier items remain unresolved realization territory; they are explicit frontier, not omitted work, and they are not reopened by this series.

XStack/AIDE contract freezing is complete, but runtime extraction, platformization, daemon or scheduler work, and repo migration remain intentionally deferred until after the playable baseline exists.

implementation_guidance_seams.statement: The baseline recipe and XStack repo-operating guidance lean on the authored MVP asset trio, but session_create.py still defaults its --bundle argument through DEFAULT_BUNDLE_ID, which is bundle.base.lab in the live registry-compile constants.

constraint_category_definitions.name: Governance / authority constraints
constraint_category_definitions.meaning: Higher-order doctrine, authority ordering, protected-surface discipline, and extension-over-replacement rules that later topology work must remain downstream of.

constraint_category_definitions.name: Build constraints
constraint_category_definitions.meaning: The compiled lane, preset family, and build-artifact assumptions that future relayout must preserve or explicitly migrate.

constraint_category_definitions.name: Validation constraints
constraint_category_definitions.meaning: The minimum honest validation surfaces, profile vocabulary, and status-language rules that later prompts must preserve.

constraint_category_definitions.name: Entrypoint / runpath constraints
constraint_category_definitions.meaning: Operator shells, public entrypoints, blocked startup paths, and live runpath contracts that must remain truthful under relayout.

constraint_category_definitions.name: Session / profile / save constraints
constraint_category_definitions.meaning: Session materialization, boot semantics, save-root truth, and authored baseline asset contracts that later prompts must preserve.

constraint_category_definitions.name: Playtest-path constraints
constraint_category_definitions.meaning: The current canonical local playable-baseline target, including loopback authority and repo-local scope.

constraint_category_definitions.name: Canonical-vs-derived constraints
constraint_category_definitions.meaning: Distinctions between canon, implementation, mirrors, audit evidence, projections, and generated outputs.

constraint_category_definitions.name: Retained-vs-deferred constraints
constraint_category_definitions.meaning: Which surfaces must stay stable in Dominium until after baseline, and which work must remain deferred instead of becoming redesign pretext.

constraint_category_definitions.name: Non-interference constraints from XStack/AIDE closure
constraint_category_definitions.meaning: The rule that XStack/AIDE closure now constrains baseline work instead of competing with it.

non_negotiable_ledger.title: Higher-order authority and stronger-source precedence remain binding
non_negotiable_ledger.rationale: Repo-structure work is downstream of canon and task law, and the authority model explicitly forbids resolving drift by convenience.
must_preserve: canon and glossary precedence
AGENTS.md task discipline
live implementation as structural truth
mirrors and generated outputs as derived evidence only unless explicitly promoted
may_vary: how the series restates or mirrors these rules
how later redesign docs summarize supporting evidence
may_not_vary: repo-structure docs, generated outputs, and chat memory may not outrank canon, AGENTS.md, or live structural evidence
violation_risk: topology decisions are made from stale prose, mirrors, or convenience wrappers instead of higher-order canon plus the live repo

non_negotiable_ledger.title: Extend-over-replace and ownership-sensitive splits stay visible
non_negotiable_ledger.rationale: The planning packet already freezes keep/extend/do-not-replace zones and quarantined ownership splits.
must_preserve: stable product anchors
runtime substrate caution
explicit ownership sensitivity around field vs fields, schema vs schemas, and packs vs data/packs
may_not_vary: split roots may not be silently collapsed
the runtime plan may not pivot around runtime/ by convenience
violation_risk: directory moves choose ownership winners implicitly instead of through explicit later review

non_negotiable_ledger.title: Internal Zeta stays closed
non_negotiable_ledger.rationale: Post-Zeta doctrine closure already states that the admissible Zeta program is complete and that the next broader mode is reconciliation and consolidation.
must_preserve: no further internal Zeta prompt before post-Zeta reconciliation
no silent widening of bounded-only, proof-only, guarded, blocked, or future-only frontier families
may_vary: how post-Zeta reconciliation is documented or sequenced after this series
may_not_vary: repo-structure work may not become live-ops frontier work or guarded-family promotion
violation_risk: relayout or cleanup work smuggles blocked Zeta realization work back into near-term scope

non_negotiable_ledger.title: XStack/AIDE remains a constraint packet, not a competing workstream
non_negotiable_ledger.rationale: The XStack/AIDE narrowing-and-contract phase is closed, and its own closure packet explicitly subordinates it to the playable-baseline priority.
must_preserve: no new XStack/AIDE prompt before the playable baseline exists
no pre-baseline platformization or migration
no extraction of baseline-critical Dominium surfaces
may_vary: post-baseline AIDE roadmap details after the playable baseline is proven
may_not_vary: XStack/AIDE contract freezing may not become permission to start extraction, repo splits, or wrapper proliferation now
violation_risk: portability aspirations justify pre-baseline repo churn or a second competing architecture track

non_negotiable_ledger.title: The verify preset family remains the canonical compiled lane
non_negotiable_ledger.rationale: Both the audit and the XStack repo-operating contract freeze the verify preset family as the current compiled baseline.
must_preserve: cmake --preset verify
cmake --build --preset verify
ctest --preset verify
the current verify-aligned build/test relationship
may_vary: a future explicitly admitted replacement lane
may_not_vary: verify may not be silently demoted
the aligned preset family may not be broken
another lane may not be presented as canonical during this series
violation_risk: relayout breaks the canonical preset family or strands the repo without one explicit proveable compiled lane

non_negotiable_ledger.title: FAST, TestX, CTest, and honest status language must survive
non_negotiable_ledger.rationale: Later prompts need one honest validation spine and one stable way to report verified, likely, partial, and blocked instead of flattening them.
must_preserve: python tools/validation/tool_run_validation.py --profile FAST as the minimum canonical validation path
python tools/xstack/testx_all.py --profile FAST as the XStack/TestX companion when relevant
ctest --preset verify as the compiled companion when the compiled lane is implicated
explicit verified vs likely vs partial vs blocked reporting
may_vary: post-baseline validation reshaping after explicit proof
may_not_vary: honest status classes may not be flattened
green validation may not be claimed without real execution
violation_risk: relayout breaks the validation surfaces, hides them behind unproven wrappers, or makes blocked flows look verified

non_negotiable_ledger.title: Python/AppShell launcher and setup shells remain the canonical repo-local operator surfaces
non_negotiable_ledger.rationale: The audit identifies these shells as the strongest operator surfaces now, and the canonical playable-baseline recipe depends on them.
must_preserve: python tools/launcher/launch.py and python tools/setup/setup_cli.py as the canonical repo-local shells for compat, profile, pack, install-discovery, and status/reporting work
may_vary: later native parity work after the baseline exists
may_not_vary: compiled launcher/setup wrappers may not be treated as the canonical operator path just because they look cleaner or shorter
violation_risk: relayout hides or degrades the Python/AppShell shells before a stronger proved replacement exists

non_negotiable_ledger.title: Direct Python server startup remains blocked truth until explicitly fixed
non_negotiable_ledger.rationale: Later prompts must not use path moves to pretend that a blocked startup entrypoint is already ready.
must_preserve: the current reality that python server/server_main.py and python -m server.server_main are not safe baseline commands today
may_vary: an explicit later fix to repo-root math, import bootstrapping, or path resolution
may_not_vary: these paths may not be promoted to canonical status through documentation, path aliases, or relayout alone
violation_risk: redesign prose tells operators to use a server startup path that is still blocked in live code

non_negotiable_ledger.title: Session materialization and boot semantics stay anchored in SessionX
non_negotiable_ledger.rationale: The playable-baseline path already depends on session_create and session_boot, and the save-root coupling is a real current constraint.
must_preserve: tools/xstack/session_create.py as the canonical materialization entrypoint
tools/xstack/session_boot.py as the canonical boot entrypoint
save_id as required identity
saves/<save_id>/session_spec.json as the current honest bootable layout
may_vary: later explicit fixes to save-root handling
later internal module grouping after explicit prompt approval
may_not_vary: the canonical saves/<save_id>/ boot path may not be broken before a proved replacement exists
violation_risk: relayout strands session artifacts from their boot path or advertises alternate save roots as supported without code proof

non_negotiable_ledger.title: The authored MVP asset trio and the current bundle-default seam must stay explicit
non_negotiable_ledger.rationale: The repo contains real authored MVP baseline assets, but the live session-create default still points at a different bundle id.
must_preserve: the existence and path stability of the authored MVP bundle, pack lock, and session template
the ability to target them explicitly
the explicit note that session_create.py defaults --bundle to bundle.base.lab while the baseline recipe leans on profile.bundle.mvp_default
may_vary: a later explicit convergence strategy for bundle defaults
a revised baseline recipe after proof
may_not_vary: the seam may not be written as already solved
directory moves may not be used to hide the seam
violation_risk: topology work silently turns an open guidance-vs-implementation seam into a fake closure story

non_negotiable_ledger.title: The canonical local playtest target remains repo-local, single-machine, and loopback-authoritative
non_negotiable_ledger.rationale: The ultra audit and XStack repo-operating contract both freeze the same immediate product priority: harden one canonical repo-local playable baseline around session creation plus local loopback authority.
must_preserve: repo-local scope
single-machine posture
loopback authority only
Python/AppShell-first operator surfaces
canonical saves/<save_id>/ materialization
the truth that the current path is a target recipe rather than a finished one-command flow
may_vary: which explicit wrapper later hardens the one canonical command, provided it composes the existing surfaces honestly
may_not_vary: the baseline may not be broadened to non-loopback multiplayer, installed-mode assumptions, or native-shell-first assumptions by convenience
violation_risk: a cleaner-but-less-proven runtime direction is chosen and called the new canonical playtest path without proof

non_negotiable_ledger.title: The local loopback runtime bridge remains baseline-critical Dominium-owned substrate
non_negotiable_ledger.rationale: The local authority path is both the strongest evidence-backed runtime direction and a retained Dominium surface in the extraction map.
must_preserve: the connection between client/local_server/local_server_controller.py, runtime/process_spawn.py, server/net/loopback_transport.py, and the current server boot/runtime path
may_vary: later post-baseline wrappers, shims, or provider interfaces after explicit review
may_not_vary: this bridge may not be extracted, migrated, or de-owned before the playable baseline exists and later review admits it
violation_risk: the current local authority bridge is moved into a portability track and the shortest proven playtest direction breaks

non_negotiable_ledger.title: Canonical, derived, audit-evidence, and implementation statuses must not be flattened
non_negotiable_ledger.rationale: The repo already has canonical doctrine, derived mirrors, audit evidence, generated outputs, and live code; later prompts need those classes to stay distinguishable.
must_preserve: canon over mirrors
live implementation over stale docs for structure
audit docs and JSON as evidence rather than law
generated outputs as non-canonical unless explicitly promoted
explicit verified vs likely vs blocked reporting
may_not_vary: audit reports, generated outputs, and stale docs may not become stronger than live implementation or canon where those sources disagree
violation_risk: a redesign packet turns evidence into doctrine or turns blocked paths into verified ones by writing style

non_negotiable_ledger.title: Baseline-critical retained and deferred surfaces must stay stable until after the playable baseline exists
non_negotiable_ledger.rationale: The audit and XStack/AIDE extraction map both freeze a retained-vs-deferred posture specifically so the baseline path is not destabilized by broad repo churn.
must_preserve: the retained status of SessionX runtime/control, AppShell and operator shells, local loopback runtime glue, pack and distribution pipelines, release/trust consumers, and repo-local operating law surfaces until after the baseline exists
may_not_vary: the repo-structure series may not do broad renames, extraction, ownership rebinding, or migration across these surfaces before the baseline exists
violation_risk: repo-structure work becomes a stealth migration or platformization pass that increases baseline risk instead of containing it

build_validation_runpath_survival_rules.name: Verify build lane
build_validation_runpath_survival_rules.status: canonical lane frozen by audit and presets; not re-executed in this prompt
must_survive: the current verify preset family semantics until an explicit follow-up changes them
notes: Later prompts may introduce explicit equivalents, but they may not silently demote verify during this series.

build_validation_runpath_survival_rules.name: FAST validation entry surface
build_validation_runpath_survival_rules.status: live entrypoint verified; full green status remains task-dependent
notes: Relayout must not hide or replace this path with an unproven wrapper.

build_validation_runpath_survival_rules.name: FAST TestX companion
build_validation_runpath_survival_rules.status: live entrypoint verified; full suite not re-run here
must_survive: the TestX companion surface for tooling/session/compat/validation changes
notes: The CLI default profile is still STRICT; later prompts must preserve honest profile semantics.

build_validation_runpath_survival_rules.name: Compiled verify test companion
build_validation_runpath_survival_rules.status: audited compiled harness; broad tree enumerated, not fully rerun here
must_survive: a compiled-test companion aligned with the verify lane
notes: The playtest tree and compiled smoke coverage must remain reachable through the canonical compiled lane.

build_validation_runpath_survival_rules.name: Launcher AppShell flow
must_survive: the launcher AppShell flow as a canonical repo-local operator surface until a stronger proved replacement exists
notes: Relayout must not default operators back to the stub-heavier compiled wrapper-first path.

build_validation_runpath_survival_rules.name: Setup AppShell flow
must_survive: the setup AppShell flow as a canonical repo-local operator surface until a stronger proved replacement exists
notes: Relayout must not degrade the setup shell into a thin wrapper with less operator value.

build_validation_runpath_survival_rules.name: Session materialization flow
build_validation_runpath_survival_rules.status: canonical materialization path; default bundle seam remains open
must_survive: the ability to create session artifacts through SessionX
notes: The CLI default bundle still routes through bundle.base.lab, so later prompts must preserve that truth while keeping MVP baseline asset targeting possible.

build_validation_runpath_survival_rules.name: Session boot flow
build_validation_runpath_survival_rules.status: canonical boot surface, but save-root fragile
must_survive: the current honest boot contract around saves/<save_id>/session_spec.json until explicit save-root support changes
notes: Relayout may not market alternate save roots as supported without a real code fix.

build_validation_runpath_survival_rules.name: Local loopback runtime bridge
notes: The public wrapper remains transitional, but the internal loopback path is still the strongest runtime anchor.

build_validation_runpath_survival_rules.name: Canonical local playtest target recipe
build_validation_runpath_survival_rules.status: target recipe, not yet one finished command
must_survive: the current baseline recipe as the top redesign constraint
the rule that later prompts may not invent a second official playtest path
notes: A later prompt may harden one command around these surfaces, but this prompt must not pretend that work is already done.

build_validation_runpath_survival_rules.name: Baseline save/load/resume assumptions
build_validation_runpath_survival_rules.status: fragile and not fully green yet
must_survive: the current save/load/resume assumptions or an explicitly fixed replacement
notes: Relayout must not break resume expectations while the baseline path is still being hardened.

free_to_redesign_conceptually: future topology options and grouping proposals
later migration phase ordering and shim strategy proposals
explicit wrapper strategy for a later one-command playtest flow
later convergence proposals for mixed or legacy roots after they are analyzed under this packet
not_free_to_break: the authority order, extension-over-replacement discipline, and ownership-sensitive split cautions
the verify build lane and the FAST plus TestX plus CTest validation spine
the current canonical repo-local operator shells
the current SessionX create and boot path contracts
the repo-local, loopback-authoritative, single-machine playable-baseline target
the truth that some paths are verified, some only likely, and some still blocked or fragile
the retained-vs-deferred baseline-critical posture from the XStack/AIDE closure packet
must_remain_stable_until_playable_baseli: tools/launcher/launch.py, tools/setup/setup_cli.py, and appshell/** as the current operator-shell center
tools/xstack/session_create.py, tools/xstack/session_boot.py, and the current canonical saves/<save_id>/ boot layout
the local loopback runtime bridge rooted in client/local_server/**, runtime/process_spawn.py, and server/net/loopback_transport.py
the post-Zeta closure boundary and the XStack/AIDE non-interference boundary
the retained status of pack/distribution, release/trust, and repo-local validation/control surfaces that the extraction map keeps in Dominium for now

explicit_out_of_scope: implementing the one-command canonical playtest path
moving files or renaming roots in this prompt
redesigning the final repo topology in this prompt
broadening into post-Zeta live-ops frontier realization
implementing a full AIDE runtime, daemon, scheduler, compiler, adapter runtime, or separate AIDE repo
performing broad extraction or migration because a surface looks portable
performing large convergence or ownership-rebinding work driven by XStack concerns
broadening into non-loopback multiplayer, installed-mode distribution, or native launcher/setup completion work
open_questions_not_yet_decided: what the final preferred repo topology actually is
what the exact migration phases and rollback points should be
what the exact shim or alias strategy should be for any future path changes
which future wrapper, if any, should become the one canonical playtest command after baseline hardening
how session_create.py default bundle.base.lab behavior should converge, if at all, with the baseline recipe that leans on the authored MVP bundle, pack lock, and session template
which mixed surfaces later belong in portable substrate, retained Dominium wrappers, or later retirement work
whether out/build/vs2026/verify/ remains the literal future artifact root or is later abstracted behind another stable equivalent
what the exact target ownership moves are, if any, across field vs fields, schema vs schemas, and packs vs data/packs
prohibited_repo_redesign_shapes: choosing a topology that breaks the verify preset family
treating compiled launcher or setup wrappers as canonical just because they look cleaner on paper
moving baseline-critical SessionX, AppShell, launcher/setup, or loopback runtime surfaces too early
using XStack/AIDE portability aspirations to justify repo churn before the playable baseline exists
promoting server/server_main.py or tools/mvp/runtime_entry.py into the canonical public playtest path through documentation only
using stale docs, mirrors, or generated outputs as stronger truth than live implementation where structural reality disagrees
flattening verified, likely, partial, and blocked into one status class
assuming alternate save roots or non-loopback transport are baseline-ready because flags or stubs exist
solving ownership-sensitive split questions by directory preference instead of explicit later review
reopening internal Zeta or pre-baseline XStack/AIDE platform work through repo-structure language
downstream_prompts_enabled: the next repo-structure discovery prompt class that maps topology, path dependencies, and ownership boundaries under frozen constraints before any topology design is chosen
later topology-option prompts that compare repo layouts without breaking the current playable-baseline path
later migration-planning prompts that need a frozen must-not-break packet before proposing shims, moves, or root renames
later redesign decision prompts that must distinguish verified, likely, blocked, retained, and deferred realities honestly
